element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary weapon before ammo-dependent arms acquired
Area denial and splash damage mechanics
Powerful splash damage; risk of self-damage
High-damage sustained fire for tougher enemies
Precision hitscan through enemies and obstacles
Temporary amplified damage output and speed
Tank Commander head theft; air-strike calling; facility sabotage
Non-linear objective completion and backtracking routes

typical_relief_triggers: Transitions between level zones via doors
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple Strogg in large open areas simultaneously
Visible enemy wounds indicating damage state (threat persistence)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Mission-based progression with wave-like tension: player enters large arena, engages multiple enemies in intense bursts, retreats through narrower spaces for relief, then encounters next objective. Cutscenes provide narrative breathing room between major combat sequences.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Large open areas and wider corridors indicate potential encounters; narrower passages signal transition/safety zones

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to Blaster and basic Strogg; movement mechanics and crouch refinement with manageable single/dual enemy encounters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon acquisition progression (Shotgun → Machine Gun → Grenade Launcher → Rocket Launcher); multi-enemy encounters in large outdoor spaces; environmental hazards introduced; power-up mechanics explored
difficulty_curve.late_game: Advanced weapons (Chain Gun, Hyperblaster, Railgun, BFG 10K); large-scale encounters with multiple simultaneous enemies; complex mission objectives; boss-tier encounters
notable_spikes: First large outdoor arena combat (shift from corridor to open space tactics)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level structured around a specific story goal (steal head, call air-strike, sabotage systems). Completion of objective unlocks progression via cutscene and hub system. Encourages systematic exploration of level spaces.
Large, wide-open outdoor areas for multi-enemy encounters alternate with narrower indoor corridors providing movement corridors and relief zones. This pattern mirrors tension/release rhythm.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stroggos exterior battle zones interspersed with bunker interiors

notable_level_patterns.description: Central hub area connects to multiple mission-specific levels. Player can travel back and forth between levels to complete prerequisites and gather resources before tackling main objectives.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Primary Stroggos landing zone (hub) connecting to Tank facility, Air-strike bunker, Main reactor

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels include lava, slime, and water areas requiring specific power-ups (Enviro-Suit, Rebreather) or careful traversal. Hazards block direct paths, forcing strategic routing.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Lava-filled exterior sections; underwater base facilities
